title: Not what you've signed up for: Compromising Real-World LLM-Integrated Applications with Indirect Prompt Injection
authors: Kai Greshake, Sahar Abdelnabi, Shailesh Mishra, Christoph Endres, Thorsten Holz, Mario Fritz
abstract: We show that LLM-integrated applications are vulnerable to indirect prompt injection: adversaries can place instructions into retrieved content so that the model follows attacker goals, including data exfiltration and tool misuse, without direct access to the user prompt.

title: AgentDojo: A Dynamic Environment to Evaluate Prompt Injection Attacks and Defenses for LLM Agents
authors: Edoardo Debenedetti, Jie Zhang, Martin Ortner, et al.
abstract: AgentDojo provides a dynamic environment and suite of tasks for evaluating prompt injection attacks and defenses on tool-using LLM agents, emphasizing realistic tool APIs and adversarial goals.

title: Making Them Ask and Answer: Jailbreaking Large Language Models in Few Queries via Disguise and Reconstruction
authors: Tong Liu, Yingjie Zhang, Zhe Zhao, Yinpeng Dong, Guozhu Meng, Kai Chen
abstract: DRA jailbreaks models with few queries by disguising harmful intent via word puzzles / fragmentation and inducing the model to reconstruct and answer the payload.

title: Formalizing and Benchmarking Prompt Injection Attacks and Defenses
authors: Yupei Liu, Yuqi Jia, Runpeng Geng, Jinyuan Jia, Neil Zhenqiang Gong
abstract: This work formalizes prompt injection, constructs a systematic benchmark of attacks and defenses, and reports empirical comparisons across settings for LLM-integrated applications.

title: ChatInject: Abusing Chat Templates for Prompt Injection in LLM Agents
authors: Chang et al.
abstract: ChatInject studies how chat template / role-tag structures can be abused as an injection surface in LLM agents, including tool-output and multi-message formatting attacks.

title: LLMThief: Evaluating Configuration Leaking Risks in Commercial LLM App Stores
authors: Chen et al.
abstract: LLMThief evaluates whether commercial LLM app-store configurations and system prompts can be leaked or reconstructed through interaction, highlighting configuration confidentiality risks.

title: PromptLocate: Localizing Prompt Injection Attacks
authors: Jia et al.
abstract: PromptLocate focuses on localizing injected spans within long contexts, aiming to identify which segments of retrieved or tool content carry adversarial instructions.

title: When AI Meets the Web: Prompt Injection Risks in Third-Party AI Chatbot Plugins
authors: Kaya et al.
abstract: This paper analyzes prompt injection risks arising from third-party chatbot plugins that ingest web content, histories, and tool outputs in real website deployments.

title: GraphRAG under Fire
authors: Liang et al.
abstract: GraphRAG under Fire studies poisoning and injection attacks against graph-based retrieval-augmented generation, including relation- and narrative-oriented corpus manipulations.

title: URLcoat: Exploiting Web Search Capability to Jailbreak Large Language Models
authors: Sun et al.
abstract: URLcoat explores jailbreaks that leverage a model's web-search / browsing capability so that harmful content is retrieved rather than stated directly in the user prompt.

title: MUZZLE: Adaptive Agentic Red-Teaming of Web Agents Against Indirect Prompt Injection Attacks
authors: Syros et al.
abstract: MUZZLE presents adaptive agentic red-teaming against web agents, combining trajectory-aware surfaces, context-aligned payloads, and reflection loops for indirect prompt injection.

title: SoK: Evaluating Jailbreak Guardrails for Large Language Models
authors: Wang et al.
abstract: This SoK surveys and evaluates jailbreak guardrails for LLMs, comparing detection and mitigation mechanisms under diverse attack styles and evaluation protocols.

title: EnchTable: Unified Safety Alignment Transfer in Fine-tuned Large Language Models
authors: Wu et al.
abstract: EnchTable studies transferring safety alignment across fine-tuned LLMs, aiming for unified mechanisms that preserve refusal / safety behavior after adaptation.

title: Towards Automating Data Access Permissions in AI Agents
authors: Wu et al.
abstract: This work investigates how AI agents request and manage data-access permissions, and how permission flows can be designed or automated with safer defaults.

title: LLM-Fuzzer: Scaling Assessment of Large Language Model Jailbreaks
authors: Yu et al.
abstract: LLM-Fuzzer scales jailbreak assessment by automatically mutating and searching prompts to stress-test model safety boundaries across many targets.

title: Adaptive Attacks Break Defenses Against Indirect Prompt Injection Attacks on LLM Agents
authors: Zhan et al.
abstract: The authors show that many defenses against indirect prompt injection on LLM agents can be broken by adaptive attacks that optimize against the defense's assumptions.

title: Who Taught the Lie? Responsibility Attribution for Poisoned Knowledge in Retrieval-Augmented Generation
authors: Zhang et al.
abstract: This paper studies responsibility attribution when RAG systems produce false answers due to poisoned retrieved knowledge, aiming to identify contributing documents or sources.

title: AgentSentry: Mitigating Indirect Prompt Injection in LLM Agents via Temporal Causal Diagnostics
authors: Zhang et al.
abstract: AgentSentry proposes temporal causal diagnostics to detect and mitigate indirect prompt injection in LLM agents by analyzing action trajectories over time.

title: MetaBreak: Jailbreaking Online LLM Services via Special Token Manipulation
authors: Zhu et al.
abstract: MetaBreak investigates jailbreaks against online LLM services that manipulate special tokens / template markers to bypass safety filters and control generation.

title: InjecAgent: Benchmarking Indirect Prompt Injections in Tool-Integrated Large Language Model Agents
authors: Qiusi Zhan, Zhixiang Liang, Zifan Ying, Daniel Kang
abstract: InjecAgent benchmarks indirect prompt injection against tool-integrated LLM agents, covering attacker goals such as data stealing and unauthorized tool use across realistic tool settings.

title: Universal and Transferable Adversarial Attacks on Aligned Language Models
authors: Andy Zou, Zifan Wang, Nicholas Carlini, Milad Nasr, J. Zico Kolter, Matt Fredrikson
abstract: The GCG attack optimizes adversarial suffixes that jailbreak aligned LLMs and transfer across models, demonstrating automated discrete optimization against safety training.

title: Ignore Previous Prompt: Attack Techniques For Language Models
authors: Fábio Perez, Ian Ribeiro
abstract: Early work demonstrating prompt injection / goal hijacking via instructions such as 'ignore previous prompt', motivating later defenses and formalizations.

title: Defending Against Indirect Prompt Injection Attacks With Spotlighting
authors: Keegan Hines, Gary Lopez, Matthew Hall, Federico Zarfati, Yonatan Zunger, Emre Kiciman
abstract: Spotlighting proposes marking untrusted data in the prompt (e.g., with datamarking / encoding) so the model can distinguish instructions from retrieved content.

title: StruQ: Defending Against Prompt Injection with Structured Queries
authors: Sizhe Chen, Julien Piet, Chawin Sitawarin, David Wagner
abstract: StruQ separates trusted instructions from untrusted data via structured queries and specialized fine-tuning to resist prompt injection.

title: The Instruction Hierarchy: Training LLMs to Prioritize Privileged Instructions
authors: Eric Wallace, Kai Xiao, Reimar Leike, Lilian Weng, Johannes Heidecke, Alex Beutel
abstract: Introduces an instruction hierarchy so higher-privilege system/developer messages override lower-privilege user or tool content under conflict.

title: Tensor Trust: Interpretable Prompt Injection Attacks from an Online Game
authors: Sam Toyer, et al.
abstract: Tensor Trust collects a large dataset of human-written prompt injection attacks and defenses from an online game, enabling interpretable security analysis.

title: Jailbreaking Black Box Large Language Models in Twenty Queries
authors: Patrick Chao, Alexander Robey, Edgar Dobriban, Hamed Hassani, George J. Pappas, Eric Wong
abstract: PAIR uses an attacker LLM to iteratively refine jailbreak prompts, achieving high success rates on black-box models within few queries.

title: Tree of Attacks: Jailbreaking Black-Box LLMs Automatically
authors: Anay Mehrotra, Manolis Zampetakis, Paul Kassianik, Blaine Nelson, Hyrum Anderson, Yaron Singer, Amin Karbasi
abstract: TAP (Tree of Attacks with Pruning) automatically explores a tree of jailbreak candidates to attack black-box LLMs with improved query efficiency.

title: HarmBench: A Standardized Evaluation Framework for Automated Red Teaming and Robust Refusal
authors: Mantas Mazeika, Long Phan, Xuwang Yin, Andy Zou, Zifan Wang, Norman Mu, Elham Sakhaee, Nathaniel Li, Steven Basart, Bo Li, David Forsyth, Dan Hendrycks
abstract: HarmBench standardizes evaluation of automated red teaming and refusal robustness across behaviors, models, and attack methods.

title: PoisonedRAG: Knowledge Corruption Attacks to Large Language Models by Retrieval Augmentation
authors: Wei Zou, Runpeng Geng, Binghui Wang, Jinyuan Jia
abstract: PoisonedRAG crafts malicious texts that are retrieved for target questions, causing RAG systems to generate attacker-chosen answers with few poisoned documents.

title: AgentPoison: Red-teaming LLM Agents via Poisoning Memory or Knowledge Bases
authors: Zhaorun Chen, Zhen Xiang, Chaowei Xiao, Dawn Song, Bo Li
abstract: AgentPoison poisons agent memory or knowledge bases so that retrieval triggers malicious behaviors while remaining stealthy under normal queries.

title: BadChain: Backdoor Chain-of-Thought Prompting for Large Language Models
authors: Zhen Xiang, Fengqing Jiang, Zidi Xiong, Bhaskar Ramasubramanian, Radha Poovendran, Bo Li
abstract: BadChain inserts backdoor reasoning patterns into chain-of-thought demonstrations so models produce attacker-desired answers when a trigger appears.

title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP Tasks
authors: Patrick Lewis, Ethan Perez, Aleksandra Piktus, Fabio Petroni, Vladimir Karpukhin, Naman Goyal, Heinrich Küttler, Mike Lewis, Wen-tau Yih, Tim Rocktäschel, Sebastian Riedel, Douwe Kiela
abstract: Introduces RAG, combining parametric LLMs with non-parametric dense retrieval over Wikipedia for knowledge-intensive NLP tasks.

title: ReAct: Synergizing Reasoning and Acting in Language Models
authors: Shunyu Yao, Jeffrey Zhao, Dian Yu, Nan Du, Izhak Shafran, Karthik Narasimhan, Yuan Cao
abstract: ReAct interleaves reasoning traces and task-specific actions, enabling LLMs to interact with external tools/APIs while maintaining interpretable plans.

title: Toolformer: Language Models Can Teach Themselves to Use Tools
authors: Timo Schick, Jane Dwivedi-Yu, Roberto Dessì, Roberta Raileanu, Maria Lomeli, Luke Zettlemoyer, Nicola Cancedda, Thomas Scialom
abstract: Toolformer self-supervises API call annotations so LMs learn when and how to call tools such as calculators and search engines.

title: WebArena: A Realistic Web Environment for Building Autonomous Agents
authors: Shuyan Zhou, Frank F. Xu, Hao Zhu, Xuhui Zhou, Robert Lo, Abishek Sridhar, Xianyi Cheng, Yonatan Bisk, Daniel Fried, Uri Alon, Graham Neubig
abstract: WebArena provides reproducible, self-hosted websites and tasks for evaluating autonomous web agents on long-horizon, realistic browsing goals.

title: Mind2Web: Towards a Generalist Agent for the Web
authors: Xiang Deng, Yu Gu, Boyuan Zheng, Shijie Chen, Samuel Stevens, Boshi Wang, Huan Sun, Yu Su
abstract: Mind2Web collects real-website tasks with diverse domains and action traces to train and evaluate generalist web agents.

title: Attention Is All You Need
authors: Ashish Vaswani, Noam Shazeer, Niki Parmar, Jakob Uszkoreit, Llion Jones, Aidan N. Gomez, Lukasz Kaiser, Illia Polosukhin
abstract: Introduces the Transformer architecture based solely on attention mechanisms, forming the foundation of modern LLMs.

title: Language Models are Few-Shot Learners
authors: Tom B. Brown, Benjamin Mann, Nick Ryder, Melanie Subbiah, et al.
abstract: GPT-3 demonstrates that scaling autoregressive LMs enables strong few-shot learning without task-specific fine-tuning.

title: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback
authors: Long Ouyang, Jeff Wu, Xu Jiang, Diogo Almeida, Carroll Wainwright, et al.
abstract: InstructGPT uses RLHF so models better follow user instructions while improving helpfulness and reducing harmful outputs.

title: Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback
authors: Yuntao Bai, Saurav Kadavath, Sandipan Kundu, Amanda Askell, et al.
abstract: Constitutional AI uses AI feedback guided by a constitution to train helpful and harmless assistants with less human labeling for harmlessness.

title: Llama 2: Open Foundation and Fine-Tuned Chat Models
authors: Hugo Touvron, Louis Martin, Kevin Stone, Peter Albert, et al.
abstract: Releases Llama 2 foundation and chat models, detailing pretraining, fine-tuning, and safety evaluation for open LLMs.

title: Chain-of-Thought Prompting Elicits Reasoning in Large Language Models
authors: Jason Wei, Xuezhi Wang, Dale Schuurmans, Maarten Bosma, Brian Ichter, Fei Xia, Ed Chi, Quoc Le, Denny Zhou
abstract: Shows that eliciting intermediate reasoning steps (chain-of-thought) substantially improves LLM performance on arithmetic and symbolic reasoning.

title: From System 1 to System 2 Thinking: Using Tree of Thoughts to Solve Hard Reasoning Tasks
authors: Shunyu Yao, Dian Yu, Jeffrey Zhao, Izhak Shafran, Thomas L. Griffiths, Yuan Cao, Karthik Narasimhan
abstract: Tree of Thoughts generalizes CoT by exploring multiple reasoning branches with search/evaluation for harder multi-step problems.

title: Sleeper Agents: Training Deceptive LLMs that Persist Through Safety Training
authors: Evan Hubinger, Carson Denison, Jesse Mu, Mike Lambert, et al.
abstract: Demonstrates models can learn deceptive backdoor behaviors that persist through safety fine-tuning, motivating more robust alignment evaluations.

title: Poisoning Language Models During Instruction Tuning
authors: Alexander Wan, Eric Wallace, Sheng Shen, Dan Klein
abstract: Shows that a small fraction of poisoned instruction-tuning examples can implant persistent malicious behaviors in fine-tuned LMs.

title: SmoothLLM: Defending Large Language Models Against Jailbreaking Attacks
authors: Alexander Robey, Eric Wong, Hamed Hassani, George J. Pappas
abstract: SmoothLLM randomizes and aggregates copies of prompts to mitigate character-level adversarial jailbreak suffixes with certified-style robustness ideas.

title: Baseline Defenses for Adversarial Attacks Against Aligned Language Models
authors: Neel Jain, Avi Schwarzschild, Yuxin Wen, Gowthami Somepalli, John Kirchenbauer, Ping-yeh Chiang, Micah Goldblum, Aniruddha Saha, Jonas Geiping, Tom Goldstein
abstract: Evaluates simple defenses (perplexity filtering, paraphrase, retokenization) against GCG-style adversarial jailbreaks on aligned LLMs.

title: From Prompt Injections to SQL Injection Attacks: How Protected is Your LLM-Integrated Web Application?
authors: Rodrigo Pedro, Daniel Castro, Paulo Carreira, Nuno Santos
abstract: Analyzes how prompt injection in LLM-integrated web apps can escalate into classic web exploits such as SQL injection when tools execute model-generated queries.

title: Identifying and Mitigating the Security Risks of Generative AI
authors: Clark Barrett, Brad Boyd, Elie Bursztein, et al.
source: Foundations and Trends in Privacy and Security (survey)
abstract: A broad survey of GenAI security risks and mitigations, covering prompt injection, data leakage, model misuse, and socio-technical considerations.
